name: Tadalafil-nitroglycerin interaction persisted through 24 hours but not 48 hours and beyond
statement: In a randomized crossover clinical pharmacology study, tadalafil 20 mg daily increased nitroglycerin-related blood-pressure lowering at 4, 8, and 24 hours after the last tadalafil dose, while the interaction was not detected at 48, 72, or 96 hours; the authors concluded tadalafil should not be combined with organic nitrates.
population_or_context: Male subjects receiving tadalafil and sublingual nitroglycerin under study conditions.
time_horizon: Nitroglycerin challenges at 4, 8, 24, 48, 72, and 96 hours after final tadalafil/placebo dose.
quantitative_note: Standing systolic BP below 85 mm Hg occurred in more subjects on tadalafil than placebo after nitroglycerin at 4, 8, and 24 hours (p < 0.05), but not at 48, 72, or 96 hours (p > 0.2); greater mean maximal standing systolic BP decreases at 8 and 24 hours (p < 0.02), not at 48 hours or later (p > 0.49).

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports time-specific BP outliers and conclusion that interaction lasted 24 hours but was not seen at 48 hours and beyond.

caveats: The study does not make nitrate co-use safe after 48 hours in every patient; label still requires medical supervision if nitrate use is medically necessary.
The study used 20 mg daily for seven days, not every real-world dosing pattern.
Emergency chest pain after tadalafil remains a high-risk scenario.
population_profile: Male monitored study participants; pharmacology directness is high but real-world risk in mostly healthy men age 30 to 50 depends on nitrate exposure, cardiovascular status, and co-factors.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 20 mg daily for seven days before nitroglycerin 0.4 mg challenges.
endpoint_measure: Standing systolic BP below 85 mm Hg, standing diastolic BP below 45 mm Hg, BP decreases greater than prespecified thresholds, and mean maximal standing systolic BP decrease.
effect_estimate: Interaction signal at 4, 8, and 24 hours; no significant difference at 48, 72, or 96 hours.
safety_findings: Organic nitrate co-use remains a contraindicated/high-risk interaction; the study supports the 48-hour boundary used in labeling.
directness_to_target_population: High for nitrate contraindication mechanics; moderate for healthy men age 30 to 50 because study demographics and real-world co-use patterns are not fully extracted.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Abstract-only extraction; monitored clinical pharmacology setting may understate risk during acute coronary symptoms or unsupervised nitrate/recreational nitrite use.
